subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Wind Chill Factor
description: A 60-second lesson on Wind Chill Factor.

hook.text: It's 10°F outside. With 30mph wind, frostbite can occur in 10 minutes. Wind steals heat.

buildup.text: Wind chill measures the rate of heat loss from exposed skin, not the actual air temperature.

discovery.text: Wind removes the thin layer of warm air next to your skin that normally acts as insulation.

twist.text: Wind chill doesn't affect car engines or water pipes—it only applies to exposed skin.

climax.text: At -20°F wind chill, exposed skin can get frostbite in under 5 minutes.

punchline.text: Wind doesn't lower the temperature. It just steals your body heat faster.

quiz.question: What does wind chill actually measure?
options: The rate of heat loss from exposed skin
How fast the wind is blowing
